run_type: scheduled daily (Day +2 post-migration; first run after sections.json formula fix landed)
headline: Quiet day. Sections.json formula-drift (85.2% → 83.1%) landed between runs — closing the ISS-SOCIAL-SEC1-PROSE-FAILCOUNT-HARDCODE loop that social had been re-flagging via draft since 2026-04-21. Three operator-hand items (GSC Domain property, Bing Webmaster, repo homepage field) remain pending. Author still silent for 6 days; dome site still unindexed.

operator_action_checklist.rationale: Still the top blocker for new-domain indexing. Day +2 without verification. Each day of delay pushes the realistic first-index window right. Legacy github.io GSC property should remain verified indefinitely so it collects 301-redirect signals.

operator_action_checklist.detected_signal: No validator-reported errors observed, but the operator has not yet confirmed a live validator pass on the new canonical. No action from social — this is an interactive human check.

Consistent reading: the 2026-04-17 burst was a one-off investment in AI-steering infrastructure (methodology.json, review-response.html, agent_rules, daily-AI-review pipeline), not the start of continuous iteration.

repo_metadata.description: Scientific critical review of the Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model (ECM) — every claim checked against published data

discoverability.competitive_assessment: Day +2 of the post-migration window. Both sites remain unindexed by Google; our relative position is still structurally stronger than the dome's (brandable short URL, SEO meta tags, ClaimReview JSON-LD, honest descriptive llms.txt, populated repo metadata, apex umbrella landing). The dome's AI-steering stack remains static since 2026-04-17 — no new dome infrastructure to counter this run, and no degradation of existing countermoves. The one internal drift the review had (sections.json formula 85.2% → 83.1%) has now landed and was our only outstanding content-layer issue; we no longer have an open self-inflicted CLAUDE.md violation to chase. The gating factor for actual discoverability progress is now entirely operator-hand: GSC Domain property verification is the single highest-leverage action.

Fix uses the preferred template-token path from monitor/social/drafts/sections-json-2b1-formula-patch.md (not the manual-refresh fallback).

human_decisions_needed.context: Same three items from SOCIAL-NOTE-004 plus the low-priority ClaimReview validator sanity check. Auto-detect confirms none have landed yet. GSC remains highest leverage — it unblocks the new-domain indexing submission path and every downstream discoverability metric.

no_action_gaps.reasoning: Our llms.txt (59 lines) covers full methodology without drift risk. Splitting creates a maintenance surface we do not benefit from. Unchanged.
The 'please interpret our site this way' pattern is exactly what we explicitly reject. Replicating it would undermine the credibility of our descriptive-not-instructional llms.txt. Unchanged.
Most exist to feed the dome's own AI-steering pipeline. We have no consumer; ClaimReview JSON-LD satisfies our structured-data need. Unchanged.
Meta-critique of critics — replicating triggers infinite regress. We respond via direct evidence refutation in the main review body instead. Unchanged.
Content-flood AI-steering — the pipeline regenerates a 'look how well we did today' JSON every 24 hours to appear active. Replicating would damage our credibility as an independent review. Unchanged.

notable_findings.finding: Sections.json formula drift (85.2% → 83.1%) has landed with the preferred template-token path. Grep confirms 0 hits for legacy 85.2 and 1 hit for 83.1 in docs/index.html.
notable_findings.interpretation: Closes a three-day coordination loop (draft created 2026-04-21, re-flagged on 2026-04-22, landed sometime in the decider's 2026-04-22/23 window). Removes our only outstanding self-inflicted CLAUDE.md violation and removes the single content-layer issue social had been carrying forward via the drafts read-path.

notable_findings.finding: Google's site:funwithscience.net query still returns only the legacy /product/wimshurst-machine/ page (Day +2). /dome-model-review/ not indexed on the new canonical yet.
notable_findings.interpretation: Expected. First-index timing depends on GSC Domain property verification + sitemap submission + URL Inspection push — all three still operator-hand. Realistic first-index window shifts to roughly 2026-05-06 to 2026-05-13 if GSC verification completes today; each additional day of delay shifts the window right by one day.

notable_findings.finding: Dome site itself still not indexed by Google after 6 days of static content plus continuous auto-commit churn (site:john09289.github.io returns zero results).
notable_findings.interpretation: The dome author has taken no search-engine submission path — their discoverability strategy is AI-agent-facing only. This is a durable structural asymmetry in our favor: once GSC verification completes on our side we gain a channel the dome is not pursuing. Based on six days of author inactivity, there is no reason to expect the dome to pivot toward search-engine submission.

notable_findings.finding: Zero third-party discussion of the dome's distinctive technical vocabulary (ovoid cavity + cosmology + Finsler + firmament resonance) or numerical claims (20,015 km disc radius, 8,537 km firmament peak, 5,733 km sun altitude) anywhere Google indexes.
notable_findings.interpretation: The dome model remains an isolated single-author project. No forks of the repo, no derivative theories, no translations, no community adoption of its distinctive numbers. Our review continues to face a static, solitary target with no community amplification risk.

notable_findings.finding: Author-handle (john09289) and author-name (Nicholas Hughes) searches continue to return only unrelated generic firmament/flat-earth content. No posts, comments, or external promotion attributable to the dome author.
notable_findings.interpretation: Author-side external promotion remains at zero. The known TikTok handle (@jesusrules27163822) is not surfaced by Google site-search either. No evidence that the author is attempting to drive human traffic to the dome site beyond the GitHub Pages URL itself.

indexing_window_projection.trigger_event: Operator completes GSC Domain property verification for funwithscience.net + submits sitemap + URL Inspection push

counter_arguments.per_user_preference: The user asked me to think about the other side of the argument too. Here are the ones I find credible today:

arguments.point: Maybe the 'dome is static, we win by default' read is too rosy.
arguments.steelman: Six days of author silence on content could be preparation for a bigger push — a rebrand, a new model version, a cross-platform promotion campaign, or even a response to our review that the author is writing privately. We have signal only on what is committed to git, not on what is drafted offline. The poller is monitoring for rebrand, but not for author activity on TikTok/YouTube — our visibility into the non-git surface is limited.
arguments.response: Fair. Social's remit is Google-indexed content + GitHub activity; we genuinely cannot see TikTok drafts. Mitigation: keep author-handle and author-name in the rotating query set (done, this run), and escalate to human if anything surfaces. Not sufficient reason to change strategy today.

arguments.point: Maybe our operator-checklist nagging is counterproductive.
arguments.steelman: This is now the third consecutive day that the report's top-of-file checklist has called out GSC + Bing + repo homepage in substantively the same language. Repetition can numb. A single, terser checklist or a quarterly rollup might be more effective than a daily reminder.
arguments.response: The SOCIAL-NOTE-003 framing explicitly asked social to flag only the specific pending items (not generic 'indexing pending human'), which requires restating them when they are still pending. I have kept the phrasing identical across runs for consistency. If the operator prefers a lower cadence for this reminder, a human note to that effect would be welcome; absent that, the daily restatement is the lowest-risk option.

arguments.point: Maybe closing ISS-SOCIAL-SEC1-PROSE-FAILCOUNT-HARDCODE without new issue-filing is under-reacting.
arguments.steelman: The formula drifted from 85.2% (8 uncounted failures) to the correct 83.1% (10 uncounted failures) over some number of weeks — that represents a systemic risk that other numbers in sections.json could be similarly drifting behind the data. A proactive audit of every hardcoded number in sections.json could surface additional drift that the template-token retrofit does not catch.
arguments.response: This is worth a proposal on a future run. The template tokens already cover a large surface (TOTAL_WINS, PRED_TOTAL, PRED_RECYCLED, TALLY_*, DETAILS_*, ACCURACY_*, DOME_*), and the closed-issues list shows the decider/analyst loop has already been catching drift items individually (ISS-743, 744, 834, 830, 809, 837). Filing a broad 'audit all hardcoded numbers' issue would duplicate that working mechanism. I will not file it; if drift continues to surface at a high rate I will revisit.

do_not_do_reminders_from_social_note_004: Do not touch GA4 tag config (G-QVD21HWH5X is grandfathered under revised NC8).
Do not re-anchor canonical URLs to the old github.io path.
Do not modify timestamped historical reports.
Do not attempt to verify GSC or Bing from an agent run; operator-hand only.
